entries.name: House of the Dragon
entries.description: It’s been boiling since the start of the series, but this summer Westeros is headed to war. Things were already getting heated between the Targaryen factions in Season 2. Out on Dragonstone, Rhaenyra coaxed more dragons out of their caves, then Alicent kinda sorta surrendered to her after a secret meeting where Alicent learned her whole world was built on a misunderstanding. Then there’s Aemond. The loose-cannon little brother to current King Aegon looks to be sitting the Iron Throne after Aegon took dragonfire to the face during the Battle of Rook’s Rest. We’re interested to see where this penultimate season goes, since this part of the adaptation seems to have caused creative friction between showrunner Ryan Condal and author George R.R. Martin. — Adrienne

entries.name: Toy Story 5
entries.description: All of the major players will return for the fifth movie in this Pixar mainstay. This time Woody, Buzz, Jessie, and the gang must contend with Bonnie’s new favorite entertainment: a tablet named Lilypad. Every Toy Story movie has a philosophical question simmering under the surface, and that remains true here. Writer-director Andrew Stanton (Finding Nemo, WALL-E) says the film is “not even really about a battle so much as the realization of an existential problem: that nobody’s really playing with toys anymore.” — Adrienne

entries.name: I Will Find You
entries.description: “I Will Find You” brings us almost to the end of Harlan Coben's 14-book adaptation deal with Netflix. Based on Coben's 2023 thriller, the eight-episode series takes on another twisty mystery, following the story of David Burroughs (Sam Worthington), an innocent father serving life for the murder of his own son, who receives evidence that his child may still be alive and must now break out of prison to find the truth. Coben fans won't have much longer to wait for his final Netflix adaptation, as his 2007 novel “The Woods” is currently in production. — Vanessa

entries.name: Disclosure Day
entries.description: Disclosure Day might not be the Jaws of 2026 in terms of its box office prospects, but Steven Spielberg has earned some of his best reviews in years with his return to all things extraterrestrial. It makes complete sense to me that an alien species would choose Emily Blunt to be one of their spokespeople, but I, perhaps like you, have stopped looking for clues and spoilers about the movie so I can simply ease into my seat and get at least one popcorn refill as Spielberg and David Koepp, one of the filmmaker’s go-to screenwriters harness their unique blend of wonder, mystery, romance, and sci-fi for the masses. — Arno

entries.name: Avatar: The Last Airbender
entries.description: We’re headed to the Earth Kingdom in Season 2 of this highly anticipated Netflix adaptation. Last season ended in a bittersweet victory when Ang and his friends saved the Northern Water Tribe from the invading Fire Nation. Now, they’re on a mission to convince the Earth King (Justin Chien) to help them battle the formidable Fire Lord Ozai (Daniel Dae Kim). Along the way, Ang and the gang will meet earthbender Toph Beifong (Miyako), a fan favorite character from the original animated series. — Adrienne

entries.name: The Bear
entries.description: After the surprise “Gary” prequel episode (and that cliffhanger!) dropped on May 5, we're heading into the fifth and final season of “The Bear” with all new worries but also plenty of hope that the series will stick the landing. After four seasons of kitchen chaos, personal breakdowns, family drama, and so much screaming, balanced by personal triumphs and enduring friendships, Carmy, Sydney, Richie, and the entire Bear crew are so close to fulfilling the dream of turning their family beef stand into a fine-dining restaurant. Now, we just want them to win. — Vanessa

entries.name: Supergirl
entries.description: Milly Alcock was introduced as Kara Zor-El/Supergirl at the end of last year's Superman, where she got caught up in some of the negative fan reactions to the DC Universe franchise reboot. So it’s been an uphill battle for Supergirl, James Gunn's second entry in the DCU, which arrives as the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger has created its own ‘verse of rumors and conjecture, including unsubstantiated whispers about Gunn potentially exiting the franchise he’s lovingly mapped out for the next decade plus. The business of it all has obscured what looks to be a super summery popcorn movie that will tell Supergirl’s dark origin story and how it bonds her with Lobo, the fan favorite interstellar bounty hunter that’ll be played by Jason Momoa. — Arno

entries.name: Jackass: Best and Last
entries.description: Believe it or not, Jackass has now been around for over a quarter century. If you’ve even read this far, it’s possible your idea of fun is watching a middle-aged Steve-O receive a prostate exam. In that case, allow me to welcome you to Summer Movie Season. — James

entries.name: Cape Fear
entries.description: Javier Bardem has big shoes to fill taking on the character of Max Cady, the psychotic ex-con out for revenge against his former defense lawyer in “Cape Fear.” First, Robert Mitchum oozed icy menace as Cady in the 1962 movie (“Children …”) . Then Robert De Niro turned the character into a savage predator in Martin Scorsese’s 1991 version (a performance that earned De Niro an Oscar nomination). Bardem has proven his ability to send chills down our spines as a villain (see No Country for Old Men), so buckle up as he terrorizes couple Patrick Wilson and Amy Adams (and daughter Lily Collias) in this limited series from showrunner Nick Atosca (“The Act,” “Brand New Cherry Flavor”). — James

entries.name: Every Year After
entries.description: The romance-book-to-screen pipeline has been booming for Prime Video. From the phenomenon that was “The Summer I Turned Pretty” to the recent virality of “Off Campus,” the streamer has the corner on the swoon-worthy series market. This month is no exception, as a new show adapted from Carley Fortune's “Every Summer After” drops all eight episodes just in time for summer break. Starring Sadie Soverall and Matt Cornett, the story spans six years and one week (they very specifically call that out btw) as two friends explore what it means to be soulmates. — Adrienne

entries.name: Power Book III: Raising Kanan S5
entries.description: Set in the ever-expanding “Power” universe, “Raising Kanan” took us back to the '90s, following the journey of Kanan Stark from idealistic honor student to the ruthless kingpin we first met in “Power.” As the fifth and final season brings this chapter of Kanan's journey to a close, get ready for a wild ride as nothing will stand in the way of Kanan's pursuit of power.  — Vanessa

entries.name: The Vampire Lestat S3
entries.description: Two years between seasons means nothing to a vampire. And there is no queer love on TV like the one between Lestat de Lioncourt and Louis de Pointe du Lac (I love “Heated Rivalry” but I mean, mortals). The storyline for the third season of “Interview with the Vampire” will go off book as Sam Reid’s Lestat transforms into a rock and roll star and some “uncomfortable things” happen to Louis, according to star Jacob Anderson. My love for Lestat and Louis is eternal, and this solo record from Lestat feels engineered to remind us humans who we belong to. I've set a reminder to re-subscribe to AMC+ starting June 7 and pause the account when the new season wraps in July because I finally did that thing where you learn just how much you were spending on streaming services. — Arno

entries.name: Masters of the Universe
entries.description: Does Nicholas Galitzine have the star power to defend Castle Grayskull from Jared Leto and turn a mega-budgeted big-screen gamble into a blockbuster? Masters of the Universe, which carries a nearly $200 million price tag in production costs alone, is a faithful-looking update of He-Man's origin story as the guardian of Eternia. We loved Galitizine in Bottoms and Red, White & Royal Blue, and Leto takes on the role of Skeletor mere months after his villain turn in Tron: Ares fritzed out with moviegoers. Director Travis Knight successfully soft-rebooted the Transformers franchise with Bumblebee; can he summon the power to get the core audience, men and boys of all ages, to show up in theaters? — Arno

entries.name: Office Romance
entries.description: You can't go wrong with a JLo rom-com (name a bad one besides Gigli ... I'll wait). Pair her with Brett Goldstein and we have the potential for rom-com gold. In addition to leading man-duty, Goldstein teams up with his “Ted Lasso” writer Joe Kelly, co-writing this charming and raunchy romantic comedy about a secret office romance between two workaholics who start thinking with their hearts. The cast includes Betty Gilpin, Bradley Whitford, Amy Sedaris, Tony Hale, and Edward James Olmos, reuniting with JLo (once again as her father) nearly 30 years after Selena. — Vanessa

entries.name: Scary Movie
entries.description: To paraphrase film critic Joe Bob Briggs, there won’t be too much plot getting in the way of the movie for this sixth installment in the Scary Movie franchise. The Wayans return, alongside Regina Hall and Anna Faris, for another story of mayhem and madness made up of horror movie parodies. It’s been 13 years since the last flick, and there are plenty of high-quality horrors for the Wayans brothers to reference including M3GAN, Heart Eyes, Get Out, Sinners, the Halloween reboots, Smile, Weapons, Longlegs, The Substance, and Terrifer 3. And that’s just what I caught in the trailer. — Adrienne

entries.name: The Death of Robin Hood
entries.description: The tale of Robin Hood is getting a dark reimagining in this A24 production. Hugh Jackman stars as the grizzled legend with a murderous past, while Jodie Comer is the mysterious woman who helps him on the path to salvation. The film is directed by Joe Sarnoski (A Quiet Place: Day One, Pig), whose next project is a live action adaptation of the video game “Death Stranding.” — James

entries.name: Leviticus
entries.description: All signs point to Leviticus being the feel-bad movie of the year, and that’s a good thing in my book. Like Bring Her Back and Hereditary before it, Leviticus is a metaphor movie. But unlike the “it’s about trauma” snoozefests that drop on streaming every week, writer-director Adrian Chiarella’s first feature faces its demons head on with a story that uses the sinister backdrop of religious cruelty to tell a story of starcrossed queer lovers. The monster here is not some vague concept of intolerance but very real violent oppression and the ways the misguided idea of “temptation” creates a destructive self-fulfilling prophecy. Joe Bird and Stacy Clausen navigate this disturbing minefield with performances that have been gathering steady praise since Sundance. — Adrienne

node.text: A Guerra dos Tronos: A Casa do Dragão
A Guerra dos Tronos: House of the Dragon
Game of Thrones: A Casa do Dragão

plot_text.plain_text: An internal succession war within House Targaryen at the height of its power, 172 years before the birth of Daenerys Targaryen.
Woody, Buzz, Jessie and the rest of the gang's jobs are challenged when they're introduced to electronics, a new threat to playtime.
A father imprisoned for his son's murder receives evidence suggesting his child may be alive, compelling him to escape and uncover the truth.

node.text: Disclosure Day - Der Tag der Wahrheit
Disclosure Day - Der Tag der Wahrheit
ディスクロージャー・デイ

plot_text.plain_text: If you found out we weren't alone, if someone showed you, proved it to you, would that frighten you?

node.text: Avatar, le dernier maître de l'air
Avatar: Le dernier maître de l'air
Avatar: O Último Mestre do Ar
Thế Thần: Ngự Khí Sư Cuối Cùng
アバター: 伝説の少年アン

plot_text.plain_text: A young boy known as the Avatar must master the four elemental powers to save the world, and fight against an enemy bent on stopping him.

node.text: The Bear: sur place ou à emporter
一流シェフのファミリーレストラン

plot_text.plain_text: A young chef from the fine dining world returns to Chicago to run his family's sandwich shop.
Kara Zor-El, aka Supergirl, joins forces with an unlikely companion on an interstellar journey of vengeance and justice when an unexpected adversary strikes too close to home.

node.text: Jackass: La Última y Nos Vamos
Jackass: La Última y Nos Vamos
jackass la última y nos vamos
Jackass: La última y nos vamos
Jackass: le meilleur pour la fin
Jackass: Lo Mejor Para el Final

plot_text.plain_text: Follows the Jackass crew as they perform their final series of dangerous stunts and pranks, marking the end of the franchise.

node.text: Cape Fear - Les Nerfs à vif

plot_text.plain_text: A happily married couple faces a growing threat when a notorious killer from their past, Max Cady, is released from prison and seeks revenge.
Two lifelong friends explore the enduring question: what if your first love was destined to be your soulmate? A sweeping romantic tale about love's timeless possibilities.

node.text: Власть в ночном городе. Книга третья: Юность Кэнена
パワー ブックIII： レイジング･カナン

plot_text.plain_text: Power Book III: Raising Kanan is a prequel set in the 1990s that will chronicle the early years of Kanan Stark, the character first played by executive producer Curtis "50 Cent" Jackson.

node.text: Anne Rice's Interview with the Vampire
インタビュー・ウィズ・ヴァンパイア

plot_text.plain_text: A vampire tells his epic story of love, blood, and the perils of immortality to a journalist.

node.text: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe
He-Man and the Masters of the Universe
He-Man và Những Chiến Binh Vũ Trụ
He-Man y los Masters del Universo
ही-मैन एंड द मास्टर्स ऑफ़ द यूनिवर्स
マスターズ・オブ・ユニバース

plot_text.plain_text: A young man on Earth discovers a fabulous secret legacy as the prince of an alien planet, and must recover a magic sword and return home to protect his kingdom.

Jackie, President and CEO of Air Cruz, runs a tight ship in her business, including a rigid anti-fraternization policy for all her employees, which is severely tested when a sexy new lawyer begins working for her.
Two friends find themselves caught up in mayhem involving killers, monsters and supernatural creatures once again.

node.text: La mort de Robin des Bois

plot_text.plain_text: Grappling with his past after a life of crime and murder, Robin Hood finds himself gravely injured after a battle he thought would be his last. In the hands of a mysterious woman, he is offered a chance at salvation.
Two teenage boys must escape a violent entity that takes the form of the person they desire most - each other.
